summary:
The school dental nursing program introduced in 1921 aimed at improving child oral health, starting with initiatives by authorities such as those in South Australia and focusing initially on urban centers including Adelaide. This early intervention sought to tackle widespread poor dental conditions observed amongst young pupils across these regions. Agent1 reasoned based on specific regional implementation details from Australian history related directly addressing public health concerns through targeted educational outreach via nurses stationed within schools themselves. 

The summary captures two main aspects of why it would have been reasonable for an initial rollout targeting urban populations given higher exposure rates & greater need identified there first before expanding elsewhere over time if successful locally validated outcomes were demonstrated accordingly per available evidence presented here regarding origins dating back nearly century ago now when modern preventive care models still rely heavily upon similar community-based strategies today too!
